daily_summary.headline: Exciting Aurora and Routine Launches on March 20, 2026

top_events.title: Aurora Viewing Opportunity
top_events.description: A strong geomagnetic storm (Kp 6.33) offers a good chance to see auroras at latitudes down to 55°.
top_events.action: Check the sky if you're in a high-latitude area.

top_events.title: Electron 'Eight Days A Week' Launch
top_events.description: Rocket Lab's Electron rocket will launch the StriX satellite for Synspective.

top_events.title: Falcon 9 Starlink Launch
top_events.description: SpaceX's Falcon 9 will launch a batch of Starlink satellites.

alerts.title: Strong Aurora Alert
alerts.message: A strong geomagnetic storm (Kp 6.33) is expected, offering a good chance to see auroras at latitudes down to 55°.
alerts.action_required: Check the sky if you're in a high-latitude area.

opportunities.recommendation: Good viewing opportunity for high-latitude observers. Worth checking if you're above 60° latitude and skies are clear.
